fields.name: Abruptic
fields.description: Having an abrupt textural change within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Aceric
fields.description: Having a pH (H2O) of less than 5.0 throughout.

fields.name: Albic
fields.description: Having an albic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Alic
fields.description: Having an argic horizon with a CEC (by 1 M NH4OAc) of less than 24 cmolc kg-1 clay.

fields.name: Arenic
fields.description: Having a texture of loamy sand or coarser in a layer ≥30 cm thick within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Calcic
fields.description: Having a calcic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Cambic
fields.description: Having a cambic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Chromic
fields.description: Having within 100 cm of the soil surface a subsurface horizon with a Munsell hue redder than 7.5YR.

fields.name: Clayic
fields.description: Having ≥35% clay in a layer ≥30 cm thick within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Cutanic
fields.description: Having an argic horizon that has ≥5% (by volume, by thin section) clay coatings.

fields.name: Densic
fields.description: Having a densic layer starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Dystric
fields.description: Having a base saturation (by 1 M NH4OAc) of less than 50% in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Endogleyic
fields.description: Having gleyic properties starting between 50 and 100 cm from the soil surface.

fields.name: Epigleyic
fields.description: Having gleyic properties starting within 50 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Eutric
fields.description: Having a base saturation (by 1 M NH4OAc) of 50% or more in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Ferric
fields.description: Having a ferric horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Fluvic
fields.description: Having fluvic material starting within 25 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Geric
fields.description: Having a geric horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Gypsic
fields.description: Having a gypsic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Haplic
fields.description: Having a typical expression of certain features (default qualifier).

fields.name: Histic
fields.description: Having a histic horizon starting within 40 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Hortic
fields.description: Having a hortic horizon.

fields.name: Humic
fields.description: Having ≥1% organic carbon in the upper 100 cm of the soil.

fields.name: Hydric
fields.description: Having groundwater at ≤40 cm depth for more than 11 months per year.

fields.name: Hyperalic
fields.description: Having an argic horizon with a CEC (by 1 M NH4OAc) of less than 12 cmolc kg-1 clay.

fields.name: Hyperartefactic
fields.description: Having ≥50% (by volume, by weighted average) artefacts in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Hypercalcic
fields.description: Having ≥50% CaCO3 equivalent in the calcic horizon.

fields.name: Hypereutric
fields.description: Having a base saturation (by 1 M NH4OAc) of ≥75% in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Hypergypsic
fields.description: Having ≥50% gypsum in the gypsic horizon.

fields.name: Hyperhumic
fields.description: Having ≥5% organic carbon in the upper 100 cm of the soil.

fields.name: Hypernatric
fields.description: Having an ESP of ≥50 in the natric horizon.

fields.name: Hypersalic
fields.description: Having an ECe of ≥30 dS m-1 at 25°C in the salic horizon.

fields.name: Hyperskeletic
fields.description: Having ≥90% (by volume, by weighted average) coarse fragments in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Hypertechnic
fields.description: Having ≥50% (by volume, by weighted average) technic hard material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Hypertoxic
fields.description: Having toxic concentrations of organic or inorganic substances other than Al, Fe, Na or salts.

fields.name: Irragric
fields.description: Having an irragric horizon.

fields.name: Laxic
fields.description: Having an argic horizon with a CEC (by 1 M NH4OAc) of ≥24 cmolc kg-1 clay.

fields.name: Leptic
fields.description: Having continuous rock starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Litic
fields.description: Having continuous rock starting within 10 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Luvic
fields.description: Having an argic horizon that has a CEC (by 1 M NH4OAc) of ≥24 cmolc kg-1 clay.

fields.name: Magnesic
fields.description: Having ≥18 cmolc kg-1 exchangeable Mg plus a ratio of exchangeable Ca to exchangeable Mg of <1.

fields.name: Manganiferric
fields.description: Having a ferric horizon that contains Mn nodules or concretions.

fields.name: Mazic
fields.description: Having a massive structure and a bulk density of ≥1.4 g cm-3 in a layer ≥20 cm thick.

fields.name: Melanic
fields.description: Having a melanic horizon.

fields.name: Mesotrophic
fields.description: Having a base saturation (by 1 M NH4OAc) of 12-50% in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Mollic
fields.description: Having a mollic horizon.

fields.name: Natric
fields.description: Having a natric horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Nitic
fields.description: Having a nitic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Nudilithic
fields.description: Having continuous rock at the soil surface or within 5 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Ochric
fields.description: Having an ochric horizon.

fields.name: Ornithic
fields.description: Having ≥40% (by volume, by weighted average) ornithogenic material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Oxyaquic
fields.description: Having groundwater between 25 and 100 cm depth for ≥20 consecutive days in most years.

fields.name: Pachic
fields.description: Having a mollic or umbric horizon ≥50 cm thick.

fields.name: Pellic
fields.description: Having ≥60% clay in a layer ≥30 cm thick within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Petrocalcic
fields.description: Having a petrocalcic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Petroduric
fields.description: Having a petroduric horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Petrogypsic
fields.description: Having a petrogypsic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Petrosalic
fields.description: Having a petrosalic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Pisoplinthic
fields.description: Having ≥40% (by volume) pisoliths in a layer ≥15 cm thick within 100 cm.

fields.name: Plaggic
fields.description: Having a plaggic horizon.

fields.name: Plinthic
fields.description: Having a plinthic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Posic
fields.description: Having a positive charge (ΔpH ≥ 0.1) in a layer ≥30 cm thick within 100 cm.

fields.name: Protic
fields.description: Having ≥35% (by volume, by weighted average) organic soil material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Puffic
fields.description: Having a bulk density of ≤0.9 g cm-3 in a layer ≥30 cm thick within 100 cm.

fields.name: Reductic
fields.description: Having reducing conditions in 25% or more of the soil volume within 100 cm for ≥1 month.

fields.name: Rendzic
fields.description: Having a mollic horizon that contains ≥40% CaCO3 equivalent.

fields.name: Rheic
fields.description: Having a fluid consistency in a layer ≥20 cm thick within 100 cm.

fields.name: Rhodic
fields.description: Having a subsurface horizon with a Munsell hue of 2.5YR or redder within 100 cm.

fields.name: Rubic
fields.description: Having a subsurface horizon with a Munsell hue of 7.5YR or yellower within 100 cm.

fields.name: Rustic
fields.description: Having ≥10% (by volume, by weighted average) artefacts in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Salic
fields.description: Having a salic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Sapric
fields.description: Having ≥60% (by volume) sapric material in organic layers within 100 cm.

fields.name: Silandic
fields.description: Having andic properties and a silt/clay ratio of ≥0.6 in the fine earth.

fields.name: Siltic
fields.description: Having ≥50% silt in a layer ≥30 cm thick within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Skeletic
fields.description: Having ≥40% (by volume, by weighted average) coarse fragments in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Sodic
fields.description: Having ≥6% exchangeable Na in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Solimovic
fields.description: Having a solimovic horizon.

fields.name: Spodic
fields.description: Having a spodic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Stagnic
fields.description: Having stagnic properties starting within 50 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Subaquatic
fields.description: Being permanently submerged under water not deeper than 200 cm.

fields.name: Sulfidic
fields.description: Having a sulfidic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Takyric
fields.description: Having a takyric horizon.

fields.name: Technic
fields.description: Having ≥10% (by volume, by weighted average) technic hard material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Tephric
fields.description: Having ≥30% (by volume, by weighted average) tephra in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Terric
fields.description: Having a terric horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Thionic
fields.description: Having a thionic horizon starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Thixotropic
fields.description: Having thixotropic properties in a layer ≥25 cm thick within 100 cm.

fields.name: Tidalic
fields.description: Being flooded by tidewater but not covered by water at mean low tide.

fields.name: Tonguic
fields.description: Having tonguing of an albic into an argic horizon.

fields.name: Toxic
fields.description: Having toxic concentrations of organic or inorganic substances.

fields.name: Transportic
fields.description: Having been moved by human activity with intact soil structure.

fields.name: Turbic
fields.description: Having cryoturbation features within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Umbric
fields.description: Having an umbric horizon.

fields.name: Urbic
fields.description: Having ≥20% (by volume, by weighted average) artefacts in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Uterquic
fields.description: Having groundwater within 50 cm of the soil surface and reducing conditions.

fields.name: Vermic
fields.description: Having ≥50% (by volume) earthworm channels and casts in a layer ≥20 cm thick.

fields.name: Vertic
fields.description: Having vertic properties starting within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Vitric
fields.description: Having ≥5% (by grain count) volcanic glass in the 0.02-2.0 mm fraction.

fields.name: Xanthic
fields.description: Having a subsurface horizon with a Munsell hue of 7.5YR or yellower within 100 cm.

fields.name: Yermic
fields.description: Having a yermic horizon.

fields.name: Acroxic
fields.description: Having an acroxic horizon within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Aeolic
fields.description: Having ≥30% aeolian material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Alcalic
fields.description: Having a pH (H2O) of ≥8.5 throughout the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Aridic
fields.description: Having an aridic moisture regime.

fields.name: Brunic
fields.description: Having a Munsell hue of 7.5YR or redder and a chroma of ≥4 in the major part of 20-50 cm.

fields.name: Calcaric
fields.description: Having ≥2% CaCO3 equivalent in the fine earth in some layer within 100 cm.

fields.name: Carbonic
fields.description: Having ≥0.2% organic carbon in the fine earth in some layer within 100 cm.

fields.name: Colluvic
fields.description: Having ≥30% colluvial material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Dolomitic
fields.description: Having ≥5% dolomite in the fine earth in some layer within 100 cm.

fields.name: Drainic
fields.description: Having been drained by artificial means.

fields.name: Ekranic
fields.description: Having an impermeable layer within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Escalic
fields.description: Having an ESP of 6-15 in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Fibric
fields.description: Having ≥60% fibric material in organic layers within 100 cm.

fields.name: Folic
fields.description: Having a folic horizon.

fields.name: Garbic
fields.description: Having ≥20% organic waste in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Glacic
fields.description: Having ≥30% glacial material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Glossic
fields.description: Having glossic properties in an argic horizon.

fields.name: Greyic
fields.description: Having a Munsell chroma of ≤3 in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Hemic
fields.description: Having ≥60% hemic material in organic layers within 100 cm.

fields.name: Hyperdystric
fields.description: Having a base saturation of <20% in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Hyperochric
fields.description: Having a Munsell value of ≥6 and chroma of ≤3 in the upper 20 cm.

fields.name: Hyperspodic
fields.description: Having a spodic horizon with ≥5% Al+Fe extracted by oxalate.

fields.name: Immissic
fields.description: Having been contaminated by human activity.

fields.name: Lamellic
fields.description: Having lamellae within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Limnic
fields.description: Having limnic material within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Linic
fields.description: Having a continuous layer of technic hard material within 100 cm.

fields.name: Loamic
fields.description: Having a loamy texture in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Manganic
fields.description: Having ≥5% Mn nodules or concretions in some layer within 100 cm.

fields.name: Nechic
fields.description: Having a water table at >150 cm depth throughout the year.

fields.name: Neocambic
fields.description: Having a cambic horizon formed in recent deposits.

fields.name: Novic
fields.description: Having been formed or substantially modified within the last 500 years.

fields.name: Ombric
fields.description: Having received water and nutrients only from precipitation.

fields.name: Oxic
fields.description: Having an oxic horizon within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Panpaic
fields.description: Having a pan within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Peloic
fields.description: Having vertic properties but not meeting the requirements for Vertisols.

fields.name: Petric
fields.description: Having continuous rock within 10 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Placic
fields.description: Having a placic horizon within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Protovertic
fields.description: Having some vertic properties but not meeting full vertic requirements.

fields.name: Raptic
fields.description: Having a broken or disrupted horizon sequence.

fields.name: Reductaquic
fields.description: Having reducing conditions and groundwater within 100 cm for ≥1 month.

fields.name: Relocatic
fields.description: Having been moved by human activity without preserving soil structure.

fields.name: Rockic
fields.description: Having continuous rock within 25 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Ruptic
fields.description: Having a broken or disrupted argic horizon.

fields.name: Sideralic
fields.description: Having ≥5% Fe nodules or concretions in some layer within 100 cm.

fields.name: Someric
fields.description: Having ≥1% organic carbon in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Spolic
fields.description: Having ≥20% industrial waste in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Subhydric
fields.description: Having groundwater at 40-100 cm depth for ≥11 months per year.

fields.name: Supraglacic
fields.description: Having formed on top of glacier ice.

fields.name: Thalic
fields.description: Having a mean annual soil temperature of ≥22°C.

fields.name: Tonguic
fields.description: Having tonguing of one horizon into another.

fields.name: Turbatic
fields.description: Having been mixed by soil fauna activity.

fields.name: Ultic
fields.description: Having a base saturation of <50% in the major part of an argic horizon.

fields.name: Vetic
fields.description: Having a very low activity clay in an argic horizon.

fields.name: Xeric
fields.description: Having a xeric moisture regime.

fields.name: Zonal
fields.description: Having developed under the influence of climate and vegetation.

fields.name: Abyssal
fields.description: Having formed at great depths below sea level.

fields.name: Acric
fields.description: Having a pH (KCl) of less than 5.0 in the major part of 20-100 cm.

fields.name: Aeolic
fields.description: Having ≥30% wind-blown material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Albandic
fields.description: Having andic properties and an albic horizon.

fields.name: Aluandic
fields.description: Having andic properties dominated by Al-humus complexes.

fields.name: Anthraquic
fields.description: Having anthropogenic aquic conditions.

fields.name: Archaic
fields.description: Having formed under past climatic conditions.

fields.name: Arenosolic
fields.description: Having properties intermediate between Arenosols and other RSGs.

fields.name: Argisolic
fields.description: Having properties intermediate between soils with argic horizons.

fields.name: Biogenic
fields.description: Having been significantly modified by biological activity.

fields.name: Biosequic
fields.description: Having formed in a biosequence.

fields.name: Calcaric
fields.description: Having ≥2% CaCO3 equivalent in some layer within 100 cm.

fields.name: Cambisolic
fields.description: Having properties intermediate between Cambisols and other RSGs.

fields.name: Chernic
fields.description: Having properties similar to Chernozems.

fields.name: Clayenic
fields.description: Having ≥35% clay in a layer ≥30 cm thick within 100 cm.

fields.name: Cryic
fields.description: Having a mean annual soil temperature of <8°C.

fields.name: Cumulic
fields.description: Having formed by accumulation of material.

fields.name: Duric
fields.description: Having a duric horizon within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Endosalic
fields.description: Having salic properties starting between 50 and 100 cm from the surface.

fields.name: Episalic
fields.description: Having salic properties starting within 50 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Ferralic
fields.description: Having properties similar to Ferralsols.

fields.name: Fluvic
fields.description: Having fluvic material within 25 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Fragic
fields.description: Having a fragic horizon within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Fulvic
fields.description: Having fulvic acid properties in organic matter.

fields.name: Gelic
fields.description: Having a mean annual soil temperature of ≤1°C.

fields.name: Gilgaic
fields.description: Having gilgai microrelief.

fields.name: Gleyic
fields.description: Having gleyic properties within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Gypsic
fields.description: Having a gypsic horizon within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Haplic
fields.description: Having typical expression of certain features.

fields.name: Histosolic
fields.description: Having properties intermediate between Histosols and other RSGs.

fields.name: Hortic
fields.description: Having a hortic horizon.

fields.name: Humic
fields.description: Having ≥1% organic carbon in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Hypocalcic
fields.description: Having <15% CaCO3 equivalent in the calcic horizon.

fields.name: Hypogypsic
fields.description: Having <15% gypsum in the gypsic horizon.

fields.name: Hyposodic
fields.description: Having an ESP of 6-15 in some layer within 100 cm.

fields.name: Isolatic
fields.description: Having formed in isolation from surrounding soils.

fields.name: Kaolinic
fields.description: Having kaolinite as the dominant clay mineral.

fields.name: Lacustric
fields.description: Having ≥30% lacustrine material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Leptic
fields.description: Having continuous rock within 100 cm of the soil surface.

fields.name: Luvic
fields.description: Having an argic horizon with high base saturation.

fields.name: Magnesic
fields.description: Having high exchangeable Mg content.

fields.name: Marine
fields.description: Having ≥30% marine material in the upper 100 cm.

fields.name: Mesic
fields.description: Having a mean annual soil temperature of 8-15°C.

fields.name: Mollic
fields.description: Having a mollic horizon.

fields.name: Nitisolic
fields.description: Having properties intermediate between Nitisols and other RSGs.

fields.name: Ochric
fields.description: Having an ochric horizon.

fields.name: Podzolic
fields.description: Having properties intermediate between Podzols and other RSGs.
